turns.content: Good afternoon, and welcome to the Goldman Sachs Group Inc. Q1 fiscal year 2024 earnings conference call. All lines have been placed on mute to prevent background noise. After the speakers' remarks, there will be a question and answer session. I would now like to turn the call over to the head of investor relations.

While we made progress on key strategic initiatives, our financial results were below our expectations. In Q1 2024, Revenue declined 16% year over year to $37.6B, impacted by softening demand in key segments. Our platform solutions strategy continues to gain traction, with asset management showing particularly strong adoption among enterprise customers. We are investing significantly in platform solutions because we believe this represents a generational opportunity. The early results from M&A advisory validate our approach. We are taking a more measured approach to our outlook and adjusting our spending accordingly.

Thank you, David. Let me walk through the financial details. Total revenue for Q1 2024 was $21.3 billion, representing a decline of 3% year over year. Our net revenue reached $33.7 billion, consistent with our expectations. Gross margin was 23.2%, reflecting our continued investment in growth. Operating expenses were well managed, and we generated $24.6 billion in free cash flow during the quarter. We returned $4 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases. For investment banking, we saw 20% growth, driven by strong demand fundamentals.
Thank you for taking my question. Can you provide more color on the net revenue trajectory? Specifically, how should we think about the sustainability of the growth rate you reported this quarter?

Thanks, Keith. Great question. On net revenue, while the current environment presents challenges, we are taking proactive steps to position equities for recovery. We are focused on operational efficiency and protecting our long-term competitive position. We expect equities to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to Denis for the specific numbers.
Great, thanks. On FICC trading, can you talk about what you are seeing in terms of customer adoption and how the competitive landscape is evolving?

Sure, Kash. The net revenue trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, we are working to stabilize them and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
Thank you for taking my question. Can you provide more color on the net revenue trajectory? Specifically, how should we think about the sustainability of the growth rate you reported this quarter?

Thanks, Mark. Great question. On net revenue, while the current environment presents challenges, we are taking proactive steps to position asset management for recovery. We are focused on operational efficiency and protecting our long-term competitive position. We expect asset management to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to Denis for the specific numbers.
Great, thanks. On asset management, can you talk about what you are seeing in terms of customer adoption and how the competitive landscape is evolving?

Sure, Raimo. The AUS trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, we are working to stabilize them and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
This concludes the question and answer session. I would like to turn the call back to management for closing remarks.
Thank you all for joining us today. We are excited about the opportunities ahead and look forward to updating you on our progress next quarter. Have a great evening.

Good afternoon, and welcome to the Goldman Sachs Group Inc. Q2 fiscal year 2024 earnings conference call. All lines have been placed on mute to prevent background noise. After the speakers' remarks, there will be a question and answer session. I would now like to turn the call over to the head of investor relations.

We delivered solid results this quarter, broadly in line with our expectations. In Q2 2024, We achieved 16% growth in equities, which is in the range we expected given current market conditions. Our strategic refocus strategy continues to gain traction, with equities showing particularly strong adoption among enterprise customers. We are investing significantly in strategic refocus because we believe this represents a generational opportunity. The early results from transaction banking validate our approach. While we remain cautious about near-term macro uncertainty, our fundamentals are solid.

Thank you, David. Let me walk through the financial details. Total revenue for Q2 2024 was $112.5 billion, representing a decline of 5% year over year. Our net revenue reached $28.2 billion, consistent with our expectations. Gross margin was 25.6%, reflecting our continued investment in growth. Operating expenses were well managed, and we generated $24.5 billion in free cash flow during the quarter. We returned $3 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases. For investment banking, we saw 8% growth, driven by strong demand fundamentals.
Can you help us understand the margin dynamics better? What is driving the pressure and how should we model this going forward?

Sure, Wamsi. The efficiency ratio trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
Great, thanks. On FICC trading, can you talk about what you are seeing in terms of customer adoption and how the competitive landscape is evolving?

Sure, Brad. The efficiency ratio trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
I wanted to ask about strategic refocus. How much of your capital allocation is going toward this area, and when do you expect it to start contributing meaningfully to the top line?

Thanks, Amit. Great question. On AUS, we are pleased with the progress on strategic refocus and continue to see steady adoption. We are being disciplined in our investments while ensuring we capture the right opportunities. We expect asset management to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to Denis for the specific numbers.
This concludes the question and answer session. I would like to turn the call back to management for closing remarks.
Thank you all for joining us today. We are excited about the opportunities ahead and look forward to updating you on our progress next quarter. Have a great evening.

Good afternoon, and welcome to the Goldman Sachs Group Inc. Q3 fiscal year 2024 earnings conference call. All lines have been placed on mute to prevent background noise. After the speakers' remarks, there will be a question and answer session. I would now like to turn the call over to the head of investor relations.

We delivered solid results this quarter, broadly in line with our expectations. In Q3 2024, We achieved 22% growth in equities, which is in the range we expected given current market conditions. Our strategic refocus strategy continues to gain traction, with equities showing particularly strong adoption among enterprise customers. We are investing significantly in strategic refocus because we believe this represents a generational opportunity. The early results from wealth management validate our approach. While we remain cautious about near-term macro uncertainty, our fundamentals are solid.

Thank you, David. Let me walk through the financial details. Total revenue for Q3 2024 was $117.5 billion, representing growth of 17% year over year. Our net revenue reached $16.9 billion, consistent with our expectations. Gross margin expanded 33.6%, reflecting our continued investment in growth. Operating expenses were well managed, and we generated $19.6 billion in free cash flow during the quarter. We returned $8 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases. For investment banking, we saw 21% growth, driven by strong demand fundamentals.
My question is on the demand environment. Are you seeing any changes in customer behavior or deal cycles compared to last quarter, particularly in equities?

Thanks, Mark. Great question. On net revenue, we are pleased with the progress on strategic refocus and continue to see steady adoption. We are being disciplined in our investments while ensuring we capture the right opportunities. We expect equities to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to Denis for the specific numbers.
I wanted to ask about expense discipline. How much of your capital allocation is going toward this area, and when do you expect it to start contributing meaningfully to the top line?

Thanks, Brad. Great question. On net revenue, we are pleased with the progress on expense discipline and continue to see steady adoption. We are being disciplined in our investments while ensuring we capture the right opportunities. We expect FICC trading to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to Denis for the specific numbers.
Can you help us understand the margin dynamics better? What is driving the pressure and how should we model this going forward?

Sure, Toni. The net revenue trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
I wanted to ask about platform solutions. How much of your capital allocation is going toward this area, and when do you expect it to start contributing meaningfully to the top line?

Sure, Amit. The ROE trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
This concludes the question and answer session. I would like to turn the call back to management for closing remarks.
Thank you all for joining us today. We are excited about the opportunities ahead and look forward to updating you on our progress next quarter. Have a great evening.

Good afternoon, and welcome to the Goldman Sachs Group Inc. Q4 fiscal year 2024 earnings conference call. All lines have been placed on mute to prevent background noise. After the speakers' remarks, there will be a question and answer session. I would now like to turn the call over to the head of investor relations.

This quarter demonstrated resilience across our business despite a mixed macroeconomic environment. In Q4 2024, ROE was largely stable compared to the prior quarter, reflecting balanced supply and demand dynamics. Our strategic refocus strategy continues to gain traction, with asset management showing particularly strong adoption among enterprise customers. We are investing significantly in strategic refocus because we believe this represents a generational opportunity. The early results from underwriting validate our approach. We are maintaining our full-year guidance and continue to invest in long-term growth opportunities.

Thank you, David. Let me walk through the financial details. Total revenue for Q4 2024 was $66.4 billion, representing growth of 30% year over year. Our net revenue reached $12.4 billion, consistent with our expectations. Gross margin expanded 25.5%, reflecting our continued investment in growth. Operating expenses were well managed, and we generated $18.9 billion in free cash flow during the quarter. We returned $2 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases. For investment banking, we saw 23% growth, driven by strong demand fundamentals.
Can you help us understand the margin dynamics better? What is driving the pressure and how should we model this going forward?

Thanks, Mark. Great question. On net revenue, we are pleased with the progress on strategic refocus and continue to see steady adoption. We are being disciplined in our investments while ensuring we capture the right opportunities. We expect FICC trading to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to Denis for the specific numbers.
Great, thanks. On equities, can you talk about what you are seeing in terms of customer adoption and how the competitive landscape is evolving?

Sure, Keith. The AUS trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
I wanted to ask about strategic refocus. How much of your capital allocation is going toward this area, and when do you expect it to start contributing meaningfully to the top line?

Sure, Brent. The efficiency ratio trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
My question is on the demand environment. Are you seeing any changes in customer behavior or deal cycles compared to last quarter, particularly in investment banking?

Thanks, Brad. Great question. On net revenue, we are pleased with the progress on capital markets recovery and continue to see steady adoption. We are being disciplined in our investments while ensuring we capture the right opportunities. We expect investment banking to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to Denis for the specific numbers.
This concludes the question and answer session. I would like to turn the call back to management for closing remarks.
Thank you all for joining us today. We are excited about the opportunities ahead and look forward to updating you on our progress next quarter. Have a great evening.

Good afternoon, and welcome to the Goldman Sachs Group Inc. Q1 fiscal year 2025 earnings conference call. All lines have been placed on mute to prevent background noise. After the speakers' remarks, there will be a question and answer session. I would now like to turn the call over to the head of investor relations.

I am extremely pleased with our performance this quarter. The momentum we are seeing is unprecedented. In Q1 2025, Our net revenue reached an all-time high of $91.8B, driven by strong demand across all regions. Our expense discipline strategy continues to gain traction, with investment banking showing particularly strong adoption among enterprise customers. We are investing significantly in expense discipline because we believe this represents a generational opportunity. The early results from transaction banking validate our approach. We are increasingly confident in our ability to sustain this growth trajectory into the next fiscal year.

Thank you, David. Let me walk through the financial details. Total revenue for Q1 2025 was $81.9 billion, representing growth of 23% year over year. Our net revenue reached $17.4 billion, accelerating the strong demand trends we have been seeing. Gross margin expanded 33.2%, reflecting our continued investment in growth. Operating expenses were well managed, and we generated $22.6 billion in free cash flow during the quarter. We returned $14 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases. For investment banking, we saw 5% growth, driven by strong demand fundamentals.
Thank you for taking my question. Can you provide more color on the ROE trajectory? Specifically, how should we think about the sustainability of the growth rate you reported this quarter?

Sure, Toni. The ROE trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
My question is on the demand environment. Are you seeing any changes in customer behavior or deal cycles compared to last quarter, particularly in investment banking?

Thanks, Shannon. Great question. On net revenue, we are seeing exceptionally strong demand and our conviction in strategic refocus has only strengthened. The opportunity ahead of us is significant and we are investing aggressively to capture it. We expect investment banking to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to Denis for the specific numbers.
I wanted to ask about capital markets recovery. How much of your capital allocation is going toward this area, and when do you expect it to start contributing meaningfully to the top line?

Sure, Erik. The net revenue trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
This concludes the question and answer session. I would like to turn the call back to management for closing remarks.
Thank you all for joining us today. We are excited about the opportunities ahead and look forward to updating you on our progress next quarter. Have a great evening.
